pages.text: Once every twenty in-game minutes or so, a $(italic)Wandering Trader$() may appear near you — a blue-cloaked villager-cousin, hood up, leading two trader llamas on tethers. He spawns, sets up shop wherever he stops, and despawns about a day later.$(br2)He is the only honest source of several otherwise rare items, and the rare gift the world hands you for nothing.
pages.title: Wanderers

pages.text: $(bold)What he sells (vanilla).$() His stock rotates each spawn. Common items include $(italic)saplings$() of biomes you haven't reached, $(italic)flowers$() from far away, $(italic)coral$() and $(italic)sea pickles$(), $(italic)red sand$(), $(italic)blue ice$(), $(italic)glow ink sacs$(), $(italic)pointed dripstone$(), and the occasional $(italic)slime ball$().$(br2)For a player who's spawned in a flat plains and seen no other biome yet, a wandering trader is a small library of seeds and saplings.
pages.title: Vanilla Stock

pages.text: $(bold)What Novus adds to his stock.$()$(br)$(li)$(italic)Supplementaries$() — the trader may carry $(bold)Flax Seeds$(), a $(bold)Globe$(), or a $(bold)Sepia Globe$() (the antique-ink variant). The globes are rare and decorative — a single one is worth catching him for.$(li)$(italic)Quark$() — he also stocks $(bold)Pathfinder's Quills$() (assorted biomes) alongside the Cartographer.$(br2)If he has a globe in stock, buy it. They almost never appear in any other trade.
pages.title: Pack Additions

pages.text: $(bold)Catching one without killing.$()$(br2)The trader despawns on his own — but the $(bold)llamas$() are yours if you take them. Lead them off with another lead and they'll follow you home. Their colours are tied to the trader's outfit; their inventories carry chests like ordinary llamas.$(br2)$(italic)Killing the trader is an option$() — drops his lead and any held items — but Bad Omen does not apply to him, and the village reputation system does not see his death. There is no penalty besides the wasted trades.
pages.title: The Llamas

pages.text: $(bold)The Red Merchant.$() $(italic)Supplementaries$() adds a $(bold)second$() wanderer — the $(italic)Red Merchant$(), a crimson-cloaked counterpart to the blue Wandering Trader. He appears in the Nether and at certain overworld hot biomes, and his entire stock is $(italic)explosive$().$(br2)He sells $(bold)TNT$(), regular bombs, $(italic)blue$() and $(italic)spiky$() bomb variants, cannons, cannonballs, fireworks and rockets, firework stars, gunpowder, copper lanterns and rope arrows.$(br2)The pyromaniac's wandering trader — and the most reliable source of cannons in the pack.
pages.title: The Red Merchant
